title: Wave 3b: decide auto-anchor scope for stock Excalidraw tools (rectangle vs arrow/freedraw)
description: Plan Task 15 ('rectangle stays glued') implicitly requires an onChange auto-anchor that stamps GeoCustomData on stock-tool-created elements. Question: should ALL stock Excalidraw tools auto-anchor, or only bbox-shaped ones (rectangle, ellipse, diamond, polygon)? Spec §3.4 defaults arrow + freedraw to scaleMode:hybrid — those depend on Task 8 (atlasdraw-375a, deferred) for correct projection. A blind 'auto-anchor everything' path creates elements that render wrong on first zoom. Decide before implementing Wave 3b: (a) auto-anchor only bbox tools (rectangle/ellipse/diamond/polygon) with scaleMode:geographic; defer arrow/freedraw to a 2nd path that activates after Task 8 lands. (b) auto-anchor everything with scaleMode:hybrid as a soft mode, accepting visual rough edges until Task 8. (c) skip auto-anchor entirely — only Task 14 PinTool is geo-aware in Phase 1; Task 15 rectangle test requires a different verification mechanism. Resolve at Wave 3b kickoff.
closeReason: outcome:success — option (a) chosen: useGeoAnchor hook auto-stamps customData.geo on rectangle/ellipse/diamond with scaleMode:'geographic'. arrow/freedraw/polygon deferred to post-Task-8 (atlasdraw-375a). Wired in MapEditor.tsx; verified via Playwright E2E rectangle-stays-glued test.

title: Wave 3b BLOCKER: selection tool can't click geo-elements (pointer-events fall through to map)
description: classifyTool('selection') returns false → isDrawingMode=false → .excalidrawLayer has pointer-events:none → events fall through to MapLibre. Once Wave 3b Task 14 ships PinTool, the FIRST user interaction post-pin-creation is: switch to selection tool, click pin to move/delete. This will fail — the click goes to map (pan), not to Excalidraw (select). Surfaces immediately in the demo, NOT Phase 4. Decision menu: (a) classifyTool('selection')=true (selection becomes drawing-mode) — only hand stays pass-through. Simple, predictable. Cost: user can't pan map while selection is active. (b) Hybrid: pointer-events conditional on whether geo-elements exist in scene. Cost: complexity, race conditions on element-add. (c) Custom 'select-geo' tool added; 'selection' stays pass-through. Cost: extra UI affordance + atlasdraw needs to register a custom tool. (d) Combo: selection captures only over geo-element bounding boxes (CSS pointer-events: auto on element shapes; none elsewhere). Cost: requires DOM mirroring of canvas hits. Resolve at Wave 3b kickoff before Worker 14 dispatch. Likely (a) for Phase 1 simplicity; revisit in Phase 4 toolbar work.
closeReason: outcome:success — classifyTool('hand') is now the only pass-through tool. selection becomes drawing-mode → Excalidraw captures clicks on geo-elements. Trade-off: keyboard 'h' or hand button required to pan map. Revisit in Phase 4 toolbar UX.

title: Verify drag-pan vs zoom element behavior matches spec
description: User visual demo (2026-05-04): R, H, Pin all work; rectangle + pin render. Reported: 'they dont move on drag but they move on mouse scroll or zoom. if expected then disregard.' Ambiguous — (a) BUG: elements stay screen-fixed during drag-pan (useCoordinateSync move handler not firing under drag); (b) EXPECTED: stay glued during drag, visibly re-project on zoom (correct). E2E verifies programmatic panBy works. Investigation: reproduce drag and observe element pixel position behavior; if stays fixed, debug throttle under continuous move events.
closeReason: outcome:success — root cause: Excalidraw layer (pointer-events:auto in drawing mode) captured wheel events before MapLibre's scrollZoom listener. User's perception of 'annos drift on zoom' was actually 'map never zoomed' because wheel never reached MapLibre. Fix: useMapWheelRouter hook intercepts wheel in capture phase and routes to map.easeTo (canonical scrollZoom math), preserving ctrl/meta-wheel for browser zoom. Verified by 4 new E2E tests: pin+rect glued under programmatic zoom (drift 0px); pin glued under interactive wheel zoom in both hand mode AND drawing mode (drift 0px). Touch pinch out of scope (pointer-event path, Phase 4).

title: Polyline geo-anchor breaks when zoom > creation zoom (line/arrow/freedraw)
description:
User report: rectangle and circle reproject correctly under pan/zoom; line, arrow, freedraw only render correctly at or below the zoom level they were created in. Above creation zoom they break (clipped/misplaced).

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS A (most likely): _projectElement polyline branch (code/packages/geo/src/CoordinateSync.ts:223-242) returns {...el, x, y, points} but does NOT recompute el.width/el.height. Bbox branch (rect/diamond/ellipse) DOES update width/height (lines 198-220). Polyline-shaped elements still carry creation-zoom width/height; Excalidraw uses width/height for hit-testing + bounds normalization; when zoomed-in points exceed the stale bbox, Excalidraw clips/renormalizes.

OTHER HYPOTHESES:
- B: bound arrows (startBinding/endBinding) overwrite reprojection.
- C: freedraw needs different handling (pressure-sensitive coords).
- D: lastCommittedPoint stale.

WHY TESTS MISSED: T17/T18 tests (CoordinateSync.test.ts, useGeoAnchor.test.ts) assert points[] correctness for polyline; do not assert width/height. Matrix passes spec; spec at code/packages/geo/src/CoordinateSync.ts:144-148 docstring says polyline updates 'x/y/points' only — spec is incomplete.

FIX SKETCH: In _projectElement polyline branch, after computing points, derive width = max(points.x) - min(points.x), height similarly. Apply same to all 3 scaleMode arms. Add tests covering: zoom-out 2 levels, zoom-in 2 levels, zoom-in 4 levels (extreme); element width/height matches points-derived bbox.

ACCEPTANCE: line, arrow, freedraw all stay anchored to geography under pan/zoom in both directions. Manual browser smoke required (vitest mocks MapLibre projection).
closeReason: outcome:success — _projectElement polyline branch derives width/height from projected points bbox in all 3 scaleMode arms (geographic/screen/hybrid). 6 dedicated tests in CoordinateSync.test.ts pass (90/90 total). Fix was already in place from prior wave; verified and seed closed.

title: Vendored fork: registerSidebarTab API — let LayerPanel share Excalidraw's existing right-panel surface
description:
User flagged that LayerPanel as <Sidebar name="layers"> creates a parallel sidebar (own trigger, mutually exclusive with Library). Asks for slotting into the EXISTING right-side panel (Excalidraw's DefaultSidebar / Library).

CONSTRAINTS in v0.18:
- appState.openSidebar is singular (only one named sidebar open at a time)
- DefaultSidebar (LayerUI.tsx:438) is hardcoded with Library content; no API to inject additional tabs
- Sidebar supports tabs internally (Sidebar.Tabs / Sidebar.Tab / Sidebar.TabTrigger) but DefaultSidebar doesn't expose them for project use

OPTIONS:
A. Vendored fork — add registerSidebarTab(name: string, component: ReactNode) imperative API. DefaultSidebar augments to render Library + project-registered tabs side-by-side via Sidebar.Tabs. Atlas registers a 'layers' tab. ONE trigger (Library button) opens the panel, tabs switch between Library and Layers content.
B. Use name='library' to override DefaultSidebar entirely — replaces Library functionality. Bad — kills Excalidraw's library UX.
C. Status quo (separate name='layers' sidebar) — works but creates a second trigger and second panel concept.

RECOMMEND: A. Same fork pattern as registerContextMenuItem (shipped in 7fcd94a) and the Phase 4 registerDialog seed (atlasdraw-50c0). Estimated ~60 LOC vendored patch (App.tsx + DefaultSidebar.tsx + types.ts + LayerUI.tsx if needed).

DEPENDS ON: User decision on whether the fork is worth the cost vs accepting C as 'good enough'.

ACCEPTANCE: Library trigger button opens a panel with TWO tabs: Library + Layers. atlasdraw's MainMenu 'Layers panel' item activates the Layers tab. No second sidebar surface. Shares trigger with built-in Library.
closeReason: outcome:success — registerSidebarTab vendored fork shipped 2026-05-05 in b8bb015 (Option A from this seed). LayerPanel now mounts as a tab inside Excalidraw's DefaultSidebar via excalidrawAPI.registerSidebarTab; MainMenu Layers item routes via toggleSidebar({name: DEFAULT_SIDEBAR.name, tab: 'layers'}). Closes T31 (CSS-module retrofit). Verified atlas-app vitest 91/91 + monorepo tsc PASS.

title: Phase 4 T5: Vendor real Protomaps light/dark + OpenFreeMap bright styles
description:
Plan §5 Task 5 Steps 1-3 (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-03-atlasdraw-phase-4-mvp-self-host.md). Vendor the actual MapLibre style JSON files at code/packages/basemap/src/styles/{protomaps-light,protomaps-dark,openfreemap-bright}.json. Source: Protomaps v4 GitHub release (light.json, dark.json) and OpenFreeMap bright style endpoint. Token \`__PMTILES_PATH__\` substitution must be preserved for self-hosted basemaps; openfreemap-bright is requiresRemote:true (no substitution).

CONTEXT (audit 2026-05-10): a previous session committed hand-rolled toy stubs (5-layer protomaps, 1-layer openfreemap → blank tan rectangle) to the working tree, never committed, never under a seed. Reverted in this session. Real T5 work is now blocked again until properly executed. T6 BasemapPicker (atlasdraw-?, commit 9cb691e) already shipped and depends on T5 producing usable styles.
closeReason: outcome:success — landed in e35fa53. Real Protomaps light/dark (68 layers each) via protomaps-themes-base@4.5.0 devDep + scripts/build-styles.mjs generator; OpenFreeMap bright vendored verbatim from tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/bright. All 4 style-builder + 6 BasemapRegistry + 2 pmtiles-protocol tests still green (18/18 with T7's 6 new resolver tests).

title: Phase 4 T7: PMTiles path resolver — VITE_PMTILES_PATH config gate
description:
Plan §5 Task 7 + §7 Q8. Implement code/packages/basemap/src/resolver.ts (or co-located): read pmtiles archive path from VITE_PMTILES_PATH env var (build-time), with fallback for dev (e.g. /data/world-low-zoom.pmtiles). Replace any hardcoded path in MapEditor.tsx style-load callback with resolver call.

CONTEXT (audit 2026-05-10): a previous session bypassed this resolver entirely by hardcoding /data/india.pmtiles in MapEditor.tsx:407 and shipping a 4.9 GB local-only file in untracked public/data/. Reverted. Without T7, BasemapPicker (T6, already shipped) cannot resolve a real archive on a fresh clone — falls through to OSM raster or blank canvas.
closeReason: outcome:success — landed in ac7f256. New resolver.ts exports resolveStyle(id, {allowRemote}), getPmtilesPath() (reads VITE_PMTILES_PATH with /data/world-low-zoom.pmtiles fallback), and BasemapRemoteGatedError class. MapEditor.tsx now calls resolveStyle with allowRemote:false (TODO: wire from app config tracked in T14/T15). 6 resolver tests pass. No hardcoded paths remain except fallback string + test assertion.

title: Phase 4 wave-misorder cleanup: T6 BasemapPicker landed ahead of T5/T7
description:
Commit 9cb691e (T6 — BasemapPicker wired into MainMenu canvas-background slot) shipped during Wave 0 even though plan §6 places T6 in Wave 1 alongside T5 (real styles) and T7 (resolver). The picker UI now exists but currently drives:
- toy/missing styles (T5 not done)
- a hardcoded or absent pmtiles path (T7 not done)

Resulting UX: opening the picker swaps to a style that may render as OSM raster fallback or a blank tan rectangle, with no signal to the user that the basemap is degraded.

Resolution options (decide before T5/T7 land):
  (a) Keep T6 shipped; add a 'basemap unavailable' state in the picker until T5/T7 close. 
  (b) Hide/disable the picker behind a feature flag until T5+T7 are green. 
  (c) Revert 9cb691e and re-land T6 inside Wave 1 to preserve plan order.

This seed tracks the decision + execution. Blocked-by: atlasdraw-T5 + atlasdraw-T7 (sibling seeds filed 2026-05-10).
closeReason: outcome:success — resolved by option (a): keep T6 shipped. T5 (atlasdraw-bdf9) + T7 (atlasdraw-e088) both landed 2026-05-10; BasemapPicker (9cb691e) now drives real styles via resolveStyle, no longer renders blank tan or OSM-fallback degradation. Wave-misorder is closed by the dependencies landing immediately after — no feature-flag or revert needed.

title: MapCanvas.tsx: remove hardcoded openfreemap-liberty default style
description:
code/packages/basemap/src/MapCanvas.tsx lines 11/35/58-60 hardcode 'https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty' as the default styleUrl. With T6 (BasemapPicker, 9cb691e) + T7 (resolver, ac7f256) landed, MapCanvas's default is overridden milliseconds later by the resolveStyle useEffect — but the initial fetch still fires (see network log from smoke test 2026-05-10: 2x failed liberty fetches before protomaps-light kicks in).

Fix options: (a) remove the default, require styleUrl prop. (b) align default with BASEMAPS[0] (protomaps-light) so the initial fetch matches the final state. Option (a) cleaner — MapCanvas is internal to atlas-app and MapEditor always passes a style.

Discovered via: playwright smoke test of localhost:5174 after T5+T6+T7 landed. See atlasdraw-bdf9/e088/ea96 close notes.
closeReason: outcome:success — fixed in cfb951e. MapCanvas DEFAULT_STYLE_URL → DEFAULT_STYLE (inline empty MapLibre style, no tile fetch, works offline). Re-smoke 2026-05-10: transformed source shows name:'atlasdraw-empty', no liberty fetch in network log.

title: Vite dev server returns 200/HTML for missing /data/*.pmtiles → 'Wrong magic number for PMTiles archive'
description:
Smoke test 2026-05-10 of localhost:5174 with VITE_PMTILES_PATH unset (or unresolved — see sibling bug). MapEditor fetched http://localhost:5174/data/world-low-zoom.pmtiles → server responded 200 OK with the HTML index.html body (SPA fallback). MapLibre/pmtiles tried to parse it as a PMTiles archive → console error 'Wrong magic number for PMTiles archive at pmtiles.js:2066'.

Two failure modes here:
1. Vite SPA fallback should NOT apply to /data/* paths — only to client routes. Configure vite.config.ts server.headers/routing or a middleware to 404 on missing static assets under /data/.
2. style-builder.ts already HEAD-checks the pmtiles path before substitution — that ladder was reverted in audit. Reconsider re-adding a server-side HEAD probe + fallback to OSM raster, OR fail-loud with a clear 'pmtiles not at ${path}' error rather than letting pmtiles.js complain about magic numbers.

Discovered via: playwright smoke test 2026-05-10. atlasdraw-320b's blob:'expected number, found null' warnings reproduced 3x in the same load.
closeReason: outcome:success — fixed in cfb951e. Added pmtilesNotFoundPlugin in vite.config.ts (configureServer middleware) that returns 404 text/plain with a clear message naming the missing file + env-var. Re-smoke 2026-05-10: GET /data/totally-fake.pmtiles → 404 with helpful body. MapLibre 'Wrong magic number' no longer reachable for missing archives.

title: VITE_PMTILES_PATH not picked up from .env.local despite correct location
description:
Smoke test 2026-05-10. Setup: wrote code/apps/atlas-app/.env.local containing VITE_PMTILES_PATH=/data/india.pmtiles, started yarn workspace @atlasdraw/atlas-app dev fresh. Resolver should have returned /data/india.pmtiles. Network log shows the actual fetch was /data/world-low-zoom.pmtiles — the resolver fallback string. Env var was not applied.

Hypotheses:
1. Vite resolves env vars per-app but the resolver lives in a separate workspace (@atlasdraw/basemap). When Vite transforms cross-package source imports, the import.meta.env transform may not fire for files outside the app's root.
2. Vite's envDir defaults to root config dir — but the resolver's import.meta is being transformed in @atlasdraw/basemap's context where no .env exists.
3. The 'typeof import.meta !== "undefined"' guard in resolver.ts:55-58 may short-circuit the property access.

Verification: build atlas-app, inspect transformed resolver.js for the inlined env-var literal. If it's still 'import.meta.env.VITE_PMTILES_PATH' rather than the string '/data/india.pmtiles' from .env.local, the transform didn't fire.

Fix candidates: (a) read env via a function the atlas-app passes into resolveStyle (decouple from Vite-specific import.meta). (b) move getPmtilesPath() to atlas-app side. (c) configure vite.config.ts envDir explicitly. Option (a) probably cleanest — basemap pkg becomes config-agnostic.
closeReason: outcome:success — fixed in cfb951e. Refactored resolver.ts to require pmtilesPath in ResolveStyleOptions; MapEditor.tsx reads import.meta.env.VITE_PMTILES_PATH directly (literal pattern Vite transforms). Re-smoke 2026-05-10 confirms /data/india.pmtiles is fetched (not the fallback). Root cause was Vite's textual env-replacement only matching the literal pattern, not aliased variables.

title: Phase 4 T12: build world-low-zoom.pmtiles via basemap-world Makefile recipe
description:
Plan §5 Task 12. Needed: a pmtiles archive with world coverage at zooms 0-6 (~50-100 MB) for the bundled docker-compose.minimal.yml default. Without this, atlasdraw users (a) need to source their own pmtiles, or (b) see the map render only where their archive's bbox happens to cover.

CONCRETE EVIDENCE (2026-05-10 smoke):
The maintainer's local /data/india.pmtiles has bbox 68°E-97°E, 6°N-37°N (South Asia only). At world zooms the map appears to render only in that geographic bbox; outside it MapLibre paints the protomaps-light/dark style's background-color layer, producing a 'dark rectangle covers half the viewport' symptom that took multiple wrong diagnoses before the bbox header was read.

REPRODUCTION:
- node -e 'const fs=require("fs"),fd=fs.openSync("code/apps/atlas-app/public/data/india.pmtiles","r"),b=Buffer.alloc(127);fs.readSync(fd,b,0,127,0);fs.closeSync(fd);console.log({bbox:[b.readInt32LE(102)/1e7,b.readInt32LE(106)/1e7,b.readInt32LE(110)/1e7,b.readInt32LE(114)/1e7]})'

DELIVERABLE:
- Makefile target 'basemap-world' that downloads/builds the world-low-zoom archive (Protomaps publishes daily extracts; can pin a release)
- Wire to docker-compose.minimal.yml volume mount at /data/world-low-zoom.pmtiles
- Document in README the env var override path (VITE_PMTILES_PATH) for users supplying their own regional archive

FALLBACK FEATURE (separate concern, optional follow-up):
Consider: when the loaded basemap style requests tiles outside the archive's declared bbox, atlas-app could surface a non-blocking warning ('You're viewing outside India — switch basemap or zoom in') rather than silently painting background grey. Tracks atlasdraw-bdf9 (T5 styles) closure: real protomaps style + a bounded local archive interact opaquely.

title: Space+drag pan doesn't reach the map (hand-tool button works)
description:
When the user holds Space and drags on the canvas, the expected behavior is panning the map (matching Excalidraw's space-pan convention). Currently nothing happens. Selecting the hand-tool button explicitly DOES pan correctly.

ROOT CAUSE (2026-05-10 diagnosis):
Excalidraw v0.18 captures the space-key gesture at the document level and mutates appState.scrollX/scrollY directly. The viewport-lock effect in MapEditor.tsx (handleExcalidrawChange, section 2 'Scroll lock') resets scrollX/Y to 0 on every onChange — which is correct for geo-anchor preservation but eats the space-pan delta before it can reach map.panBy.

FIX SKETCH:
Before resetting scrollX/Y to 0 in the watchdog, compute the delta (dx = appState.scrollX, dy = appState.scrollY, since prev was 0 after the last reset) and call map.panBy([-dx, -dy], { animate: false }). Guard against scrollToContent-driven scroll changes on initial load (track a 'has user-pan happened yet' ref keyed on space-key state, or distinguish by whether elements changed).

ALTERNATIVE: handle space+drag at the document level ourselves (window.addEventListener('keydown', ...)). Bypass Excalidraw entirely.

WORKAROUND: hand-tool button (top-left toolbar, position 2) works correctly.

title: Phase 4 T2b: atlas-app AppConfig with VITE_BUILD_TARGET discriminator (pages|local-only|hosted)
description:
Wave 0 dependency added by 2026-05-10 plan scrub note on §5 Task 2.

Creates the frontend deployment-mode discriminator that splits the build into a casual-tier (static GH Pages) and a power-tier (docker self-host). Sibling to T2 (backend storage config), distinct file: code/apps/atlas-app/src/config/app-config.ts.

Spec (per scrub note in plan §5 Task 2):
1. Zod schema for VITE_BUILD_TARGET in { 'pages' | 'local-only' | 'hosted' }, default 'pages'.
2. loadAppConfig() that throws with a named-var error on parse failure.
3. Feature-flag exports: ENABLE_SHARE_UI, ENABLE_REALTIME, ENABLE_BACKEND_PERSISTENCE.
4. Vite base setting wired off VITE_BUILD_TARGET === 'pages' -> "/atlasdraw/"; else /.
5. AboutDialog (T14) consumes this to render demo-edition badge.
6. Unit test for all three build targets + missing-var case.

Blocks T4 share endpoint, T8/T9 share-link client, T14 AboutDialog, any GH Pages deploy workflow.

Pre-dispatch scrub required per mx-d4f376 / mx-cb3eb8 / mx-04ac8d before T2b worker is launched.
